content: JSONL conflict resolution in tryAutoResolve: resolveJsonlConflict() collects lines from all conflict sides plus non-conflict sections, parses as JSON (returning null on any parse failure to fall through), deduplicates by id keeping later recorded_at, sorts ascending by recorded_at, appends no-id records at end. Exported for unit testing. tryAutoResolve dispatches via file.endsWith('.jsonl').

legio worktree clean --completed deletes both worktrees AND their branches. Always merge builder/lead branches into main (legio merge) before cleaning. Check for pending merge_ready mail addressed to orchestrator/coordinator before running worktree clean.

description: Migration logic with multiple ALTER-or-recreate paths should update boolean flags after each ALTER to avoid stale variable fall-through
resolution: Re-query schema or update captured booleans after each ALTER TABLE in migrateSchema()

content: Coordinator merge protocol: after any auto-resolve or higher tier merge, ALWAYS run quality gates (vitest run, tsc --noEmit) before merging the next branch. Never batch merges on files that overlap — merge one, verify, then proceed. Treat auto-resolve as a yellow flag requiring inspection (git diff HEAD~1), not a green light.
When fixing merge-artifact duplicate fields (interface props, SQL columns, object keys), always verify INSERT SELECT column counts match (n INSERT columns = n SELECT values).
Migration INSERT SELECT must use dynamic select variables (like audienceSelect) rather than hardcoded literals when the source table may or may not have the column.

description: Auto-resolve merge can create duplicate fields across multiple files (types, store, commands) when two branches both add the same column/field. The legio CLI itself breaks if the merged source has redeclarations (esbuild TransformError).
resolution: Fix must cover ALL files with tsc --noEmit to find all duplicate declarations, not just the ones mentioned in the initial report. Use the installed CLI binary (separate build) when source is broken.

content: isBranchMerged pattern: use runCommand (not runGit) for git merge-base --is-ancestor because exit code 1 is a valid 'not merged' response, not an error. runGit throws on non-zero exit so it cannot be used here.

removeWorktree safety pattern: when forceBranch is false and git branch -d fails (unmerged branch), throw WorktreeError instead of silently swallowing. When forceBranch is true, keep silent catch (best-effort). This surfaces unmerged work to callers rather than hiding it.

isBranchMerged test for post-merge case: use git worktree remove --force directly (not removeWorktree()) to detach the worktree without deleting the branch ref, then git merge the branch. removeWorktree() with forceBranch:true deletes the branch, leaving nothing to merge.

When parallel builders have cross-file dependencies (imports, type references), the branch creating new exports must be merged before the branch importing them. Reviewers cannot verify cross-branch imports in isolation. Merge order matters for both additions and removals.
